question.text: What is KYC in the context of mutual fund investments?

answer.text: Keep Your Cash - a savings scheme
Know Your Customer - a process to verify the identity and address of an investor
Know Your Capital - a method to track investment returns
A tax form required for mutual fund investment

explanation.text: **The Logic:** KYC (Know Your Customer) is a mandatory process to verify the identity and address of investors before they can invest in mutual funds. It involves submitting identity proof (like PAN card, Aadhaar) and address proof. KYC is a one-time process - once KYC compliant, the investor can invest with any mutual fund house. **The Trap:** KYC is mandatory for ALL mutual fund investors regardless of the investment amount. It is not a tax form. Once completed, it is valid across all fund houses through the centralized KYC (CKYC) system. **Cross-Exam Context:** Also tested in V-A and Series XXIV. Very fundamental concept. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Help retired investors complete their KYC smoothly. Many retirees may not be familiar with online processes. Assist them with document submission and ensure their KYC status is registered on the CKYC system.
